data.title: Japanese listed company Value Creation plans to increase its holdings of BTC worth about $700,000 as reserve assets
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Value Creation (stock code 9238), a Japanese TOPIX-listed company, plans to increase its holdings of about 100 million yen (about $700,000) of Bitcoin (BTC) in the next four months as part of its balance sheet reserves. The company has previously purchased about 200 million yen (1.4 million U.S. dollars) of BTC, with the funds coming from surplus capital for future investment purposes.
Although the scale of this increase is limited, it marks the trend of Japanese companies adopting Bitcoin as a corporate reserve asset. Many Japanese companies, including Remixpoint, NEXON and Metaplanet, have included BTC in their asset allocation. Metaplanet currently holds 4,525 BTC, making it the largest listed company in Japan.

data.title: Bitcoin Price’s Breakout From This Key Level Will Cause Altcoins To Go Nuclear: Analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin price is approaching a critical trendline breakout that could trigger major growth in the altcoin market, according to analyst Dan Gambardello. In his recent analysis, Gambardello looks at Bitcoin’s weekly chart patterns focusing on the RSI momentum oscillator breaking a multi-month trendline.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/04/15/bitcoin-price-breakout-from-this-key-level-will-cause-altcoins-to-go-nuclear-analyst/

data.title: Bitcoin Indicator Flashing Bullish for First Time in 18 Weeks, Says Analyst Who Called May 2021 Crypto Collapse
data.description:
A crypto analyst who nailed the 2021 Bitcoin market meltdown says that a BTC indicator is suddenly flashing bullish. Pseudonymous analyst Dave the Wave tells his 149,300 followers on the social media platform X that Bitcoin’s weekly logarithmic moving average convergence divergence (LMACD) histogram indicator is starting to strengthen, signaling a possible rally.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/04/15/bitcoin-indicator-flashing-bullish-for-first-time-in-18-weeks-says-analyst-who-called-may-2021-crypto-collapse/

data.title: Bitcoin Price Fails To Launch With $751 Million In Outflows, Are Institutions Cashing Out?
data.description:
The Bitcoin price continues to face headwinds, as the latest report on Digital Asset Fund Flows shows a staggering $751 million in outflows from the digital asset. The sheer volume of this withdrawal raises alarm bells about whether institutions may be cashing out from the flagship cryptocurrency. Bitcoin Price Faces Pressure Amid Massive Outflows CoinShares’ weekly report on Digital Asset Fund Flows has disclosed a massive $795 million in outflows from the crypto market—shockingly, $751 million of which came from Bitcoin alone. This mass exodus marks one of the largest single-week outflows of the year, and it comes at a time when the price of Bitcoin has hit a wall
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-sees-massive-outflows/

data.title: OKX announces entry into the US market and will establish regional headquarters in California
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News OKX announced that it has officially entered the US market and will set up a regional headquarters in California. Officials said that it will launch a centralized trading platform and OKX Wallet for US users. Former OKCoin users will be migrated to the OKX platform, and new user access will be carried out in stages, and it is expected to start nationwide promotion later this year. OKX said it will actively cooperate with US regulators to promote compliance operations in accordance with current regulations, and has established a compliance system covering KYC, anti-money laundering (AML), risk assessment and transaction monitoring mechanisms.

data.title: SEC Completes Two-Year Review of Coinbase, No Amendments or Restatements Required
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has completed a long review of Coinbase's financial disclosures and ultimately did not require the company to modify or restate relevant documents. Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal said the review covered 10-K documents for fiscal years 2022 and 2023, which lasted more than two years and had fully resolved relevant comments with the SEC's Corporate Finance Department. In the letter, the SEC reminded that the company and management are still responsible for the accuracy and adequacy of the disclosures, and the completion of the review does not represent official endorsement. The process began shortly after Coinbase went public in April 2021, as compliance standards for crypto companies continued to evolve. In addition, since the Trump administration came to power, the U.S. regulatory attitude has become more relaxed, and the SEC has withdrawn lawsuits against a number of crypto companies and has begun to develop a new industry regulatory framework. (cryptoslate)

data.title: Bitcoin Miners Are Selling More BTC to Make Ends Meet: CryptoQuant
data.description:
Bitcoin miners are dumping more coins to keep their heads above water, said CryptoQuant, citing the depressed price and growing difficulty.
source: https://decrypt.co/314992/bitcoin-miners-selling-more-btc-cryptoquant
